frame.baseline_today: Support triage is manual and inconsistent.
frame.desired_improvement: Reduce first-response delay and improve routing correctness without increasing operational risk.
frame.decision_statement: Choose one routing approach to prototype next.

altitude.statement: All options are solution approaches for the same support-routing opportunity.

criteria.name: Keeps customer data handling within approved systems
Is fast to validate with a prototype
Is easy for agents to understand and override

options.name: Rules-based router
options.description: Route tickets with explicit rules based on product, plan, and issue type.
assumptions: Issue taxonomy is good enough for an initial prototype.

options.name: LLM-assisted classifier
options.description: Use an LLM to classify incoming tickets and recommend a queue.
assumptions: PII controls and review flow are accepted.

options.name: Manual queue cleanup
options.description: Keep routing manual but improve queue hygiene with batch review.
assumptions: Ops team has capacity for daily cleanup.

scores.aspect_note: Handles the most common product/plan/issue combinations directly.
Uses existing support platform fields and permissions.
Can be piloted with a small rule set.
Agents can inspect and override rules.
Likely covers more ambiguous cases than rules.
Needs policy review for data handling and logging.
Prototype is possible but requires guardrails.
Improves cleanup but does not route most cases at intake.
Can start immediately with a checklist.
